message:
feat(client): CLIENT TRACKING + CLIENT REPLY + FUNCTION RESTORE atomicity

Adds full Redis-7 parity for two entangled subcommand families —
CLIENT TRACKING and CLIENT REPLY — that need a coordinated change
across the store (state types), the command layer (parse/dispatch),
the runtime (per-session plumbing), and the server (network-reply
suppression). Also fixes a non-atomic branch in Store::function_restore
that was observable from upstream parity.

crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs
  * New `ClientTrackingState` struct (pub): `enabled`, `redirect:
    Option<u64>`, `bcast`, `optin`, `optout`, `noloop`, and
    `prefixes: BTreeSet<Vec<u8>>`. Models the full Redis-7
    client-side-caching option surface so the dispatch layer can
    read it in O(1) per command.
  * New `ClientReplyState` struct (pub): `off`, `skip_next`,
    `suppress_current_response`. Captures the CLIENT REPLY state
    machine (OFF / SKIP / ON) the server uses to decide whether to
    flush a reply frame.
  * `DispatchClientContext` gains `client_tracking: ClientTrackingState`
    and `client_reply: ClientReplyState` fields + default init so
    every command dispatch has this context available.
  * Store::function_restore idempotency fix: the prior implementation
    `self.function_libraries.clear()` *before* parsing the incoming
    count+entries, so a malformed payload would leave the store with
    libraries silently wiped. Fix: parse the count first, collect
    restored libraries into a `restored_libraries: HashMap<…>` scratch
    map, validate every library (engine presence, name uniqueness
    via `seen_names: HashSet`), and only swap at the end. Append
    mode (`append=true`) seeds `seen_names` from the existing
    libraries so "ERR Library 'X' already exists" is emitted
    BEFORE the atomic swap — matches Redis behavior and prevents
    partial restore state leaks.

crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs (+597)
  * New string constants for exact Redis-7 error replies:
      CLIENT_TRACKING_REDIRECT_MISSING,
      CLIENT_TRACKING_PREFIX_REQUIRES_BCAST,
      CLIENT_TRACKING_OPTIN_OPTOUT_CONFLICT,
      CLIENT_TRACKING_BCAST_OPT_CONFLICT,
      CLIENT_CACHING_REQUIRES_TRACKING,
      CLIENT_CACHING_YES_REQUIRES_OPTIN,
      CLIENT_CACHING_NO_REQUIRES_OPTOUT.
  * New pub functions consumed by fr-runtime:
      - `apply_client_reply_state(argv, reply) -> Result<(), CommandError>`
        mutates the per-client ClientReplyState according to
        CLIENT REPLY OFF / SKIP / ON semantics; called at dispatch
        entry.
      - `parse_client_tracking_state(argv, current) -> Result<…>`
        validates option combos (BCAST alone, OPTIN/OPTOUT
        exclusivity, PREFIX requiring BCAST) before applying.
      - `validate_client_caching_mode(argv, state) -> Result<…>`
        enforces "CACHING requires OPTIN/OPTOUT" and mode-specific
        YES/NO compatibility.
      - `client_tracking_getredir_value(state) -> i64` for
        CLIENT GETREDIR.
      - `client_trackinginfo_frame(state) -> RespFrame` builds
        the CLIENT TRACKINGINFO reply array.
  * `client_tracking_flags(state)` helper produces the
    ["on","bcast","optin","optout","noloop"] reply array used by
    CLIENT INFO / CLIENT LIST — or ["off"] when disabled.
  * dispatch_argv now calls
    `apply_client_reply_state(argv, &mut store.dispatch_client_ctx.client_reply)?`
    at the top so every command frame updates the per-client reply
    state before routing — matching how Redis handles it.
  * Imports `{ClientReplyState, ClientTrackingState}` from fr-store;
    adds `BTreeSet` to the std::collections import for prefix sets.

crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs
  * ClientSession gains `client_tracking: ClientTrackingState` and
    `client_reply: ClientReplyState`. Reset on disconnect / auth
    refresh alongside the existing client_name/lib_name/no_evict
    state.
  * New public method `Runtime::suppress_current_network_reply()`
    the server calls to decide whether to flush the reply buffer
    for the most recent command — honors CLIENT REPLY OFF and
    the "skip next" transient state.
  * Wires the parse/apply helpers from fr-command into the
    CLIENT TRACKING / CACHING / GETREDIR / TRACKINGINFO / REPLY
    dispatch paths.
  * Imports expand: `{ClientReplyState, ClientTrackingState}` from
    fr-store plus `{apply_client_reply_state,
    client_tracking_getredir_value, client_trackinginfo_frame,
    parse_client_tracking_state, validate_client_caching_mode}`
    from fr-command.

crates/fr-server/src/main.rs
  * `suppress_client_network_reply(runtime, frame, response)` now
    takes `&Runtime` as its first argument and consults
    `runtime.suppress_current_network_reply()` as the primary gate
    before the existing REPLCONF ACK/GETACK internal-control
    check. The test module grows a new unit test,
    `client_reply_off_suppresses_network_replies_until_on`,
    exercising the CLIENT REPLY OFF → SKIP → ON state machine
    against a real ClientConnection + Runtime via mio Token.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
